title: My Stanford Mentor - John Shoven
content: John Shoven was my dissertation advisor at Stanford. Brilliant guy - he's the Charles R. Schwab Professor of Economics there. But what made John special wasn't just his intellect. He cared about real-world impact. We'd have these long debates about tax efficiency, and he'd always push me: 'What does this mean for the person at the kitchen table?' We ended up co-authoring 'Ranking Mutual Funds on an After-Tax Basis' together. Our research showed taxes could eat 25% of mutual fund returns. That number shocked people. Changed how the industry thought about after-tax performance.
follow_up: John taught me something I still carry: the best research answers questions real people have. Not abstract puzzles - real problems. Every time I publish something now, I ask myself: would this help someone at their kitchen table? That's John's voice in my head.
